channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Safari Rally Kenya 2026 kicks off in Naivasha with public shakedown and lively local festivities
item.description: Day one of the 2026 Safari Rally Kenya, the third round of the World Rally Championship, saw Naivasha town in Nakuru County transform into a hub of high-octane motorsport action. The rally began with ...

item.title: Iran Conflict Affects LPG Supply, Indian Families Adjust Wedding Plans Accordingly
item.description: The ongoing tension in Iran, particularly around the Hormuz region, is impacting LPG (liquefied petroleum gas) supplies to India. Families preparing for weddings are facing a dilemma: either they secu...

item.title: Three Spotted Gully Sharks Returned to Eastern Cape Waters for Research
item.description: Three spotted gully sharks that had outgrown the Two Oceans Aquarium’s Kelp Forest Exhibit have been released back into the wild off the Eastern Cape coast, near Mgwalana. The release forms part of a ...

item.title: Investigation underway after two deaths linked to plasma donation in Winnipeg
item.description: Authorities in Winnipeg are investigating the deaths of two individuals who recently donated plasma at a local clinic. The incidents have raised concerns about the safety protocols and medical oversig...

item.title: Kelly Clarkson Reveals She Never Received Her American Idol Season 1 Prizes
item.description: Kelly Clarkson, winner of the inaugural season of American Idol in 2002, has revealed that she never received the prizes promised to her for winning the show. Speaking on The Kelly Clarkson Show with ...

item.title: House passes bill setting BARMM parliamentary elections in September 2026
item.description: COTABATO CITY — Presidential Peace Adviser Carlito Galvez Jr. expressed his appreciation to the House of Representatives for approving the bill that resets the first regular parliamentary elections in...

item.title: How Afrobeats Music Reflects Cybercrime Culture and Its Global Reach
item.description: Dis article explain how Afrobeats, the popular music from Nigeria and West Africa, dey sometimes glamorize cybercrime. Researchers study 40 songs from 2023 to 2025 and find say some lyrics dey praise ...

item.title: TPDC Implements Major Projects to Increase Tanzania’s Gas Output
item.description: Dodoma: Tanzania Petroleum Development Corporation (TPDC) has launched several big projects to make sure the country’s natural gas production remains strong and even increases. TPDC Director General M...

item.title: Army recovers nearly 300 hand grenades from abandoned terrorist hideout in Lanao del Sur
item.description: Soldiers of the Philippine Army’s 55th Infantry Battalion discovered 295 hand grenades in an abandoned camp of the local terrorist group Dawlah Islamiya in Barangay Piagolongan, Marogong, Lanao del Su...

item.title: Rear Admiral Okehie Highlights Role of Inter-House Sports in Youth Development
item.description: Rear Admiral Chiedozie Okehie, Flag Officer Commanding Eastern Naval Command, tok say inter-house sports competition na main way to discover talents from grassroots. E talk say dis kind competition do...

item.title: Several Kapsabet School Pupils Injured After Old Septic Tank Collapses in School Compound
item.description: A group of 11 pupils from a school in Kapsabet, Nandi County, were injured after an old septic tank within the school compound suddenly collapsed beneath them. The incident happened as the learners we...

item.title: US Tech AI Investments in Gulf States Face Risks Amid Regional Conflicts
item.description: The United States’ major technology firms, including Amazon, Microsoft, and Google, have invested heavily in building AI data centres across the Gulf region, aiming to establish it as a global hub for...

item.title: False therapist jailed for 11 years for sexually assaulting and deceiving patient under guise of treatment
item.description: A 77-year-old man named Gerald Peck has been sentenced to 11 years in prison at Lewes Crown Court after being convicted of three counts of sexual assault and two counts of assault by penetration. Peck...

item.title: Tunisian Director Kaouther Ben Hania’s Film ‘The Voice of Hind Rajab’ Portrays Gaza Crisis Through Red Crescent Volunteers
item.description: The Voice of Hind Rajab, a film by Tunisian director Kaouther Ben Hania, has captured global attention after premiering at the Venice Film Festival in 2025 with a 23-minute standing ovation and seven ...

item.title: Housing tax breaks heavily favour wealthy electorates, costing billions
item.description: Property tax concessions in Australia, including the 50 per cent capital gains tax (CGT) discount and negative gearing, are projected to cost the federal budget over $200 billion in the next decade. R...

item.title: Preliminary report suggests outdated US intelligence likely caused deadly strike on Iranian school
item.description: A preliminary investigation has indicated that the United States military was 'likely responsible' for a missile strike on Shajareh Tayyebeh Elementary School in Minab, southern Iran, which killed mor...

item.title: ANC distances itself from Bernice Swartz’s bread donation campaign
item.description: The African National Congress (ANC) has publicly reprimanded Deputy Minister of Forestry, Fisheries and the Environment, Bernice Swartz, following her recent bread donation campaign, which has drawn c...

item.title: Africa's Opportunity to Grow Manufacturing and the Structural Challenges It Faces
item.description: Despite Africa's strong population growth, favourable economic projections, and a global reshoring trend, the continent remains largely absent from the global manufacturing boom. Africa represents 18....

item.title: Pakistan and Afghanistan agree to temporary ceasefire at Torkham to retrieve body of Afghan national amid ongoing border clashes
item.description: On Thursday, border forces of Pakistan and Afghanistan agreed to a short break in their ongoing clashes at the Torkham border crossing in order to identify and retrieve a dead body that had been lying...

item.title: Carlton Blues edge Richmond in nail-biting AFL clash
item.description: Carlton managed to recover their momentum with a narrow win over Richmond, 10.15 (75) to 9.17 (71), in a thrilling AFL clash at the MCG with over 74,000 fans in attendance. Despite not scoring a goal ...
